channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 60 replies — 240.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
You should be holding at least 1 BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-05-14 10:18 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62228391
>>62225044
Maybe 1k lol

62228607
>>62228389
More like native staking / BTCFi infrastructure where the asset can participate in security, collateral, or liquidity systems instead of just sitting untouched in cold storage forever.

62228616
>>62225050
you're poor if you're only earning $5 per hour

62228631
>>62222594
>wantcum cumpooping
what

62228635
>>62226434
No, i was being a dick and suggesting that some things can just go downwards

62228803
>>62220146
Yeah probably if you think long term and keep self custody. You not considering staking part of it on Babylon though instead of letting all 5 BTC sit there forever?

62228807
>>62228635
I'm just being sarcastic though

62228809
>>62225050
Just keep working hard and you're gonna make it anon

62228821
>>62228803
No way in hell am I gonna risk my bitcoin for some pennies on some centralized exchange.

62228833
Btc is Epstein pedo coin…it’s all a con, they were pissing their pants over xrp

Satan coin = BTC
God coin = XRP

62228837
>>62228833
Satan why?

62228839
>>62228821
too risky

62228849
>>62228809
What's the minimum salary for a man

62229042
>>62228821
I think you misunderstood me anon. I don’t like the centralized exchange model either, which is why the self-custody side of BTCFi is way more interesting.

62229048
>>62222489
Why LTC?

62229075
>>62228833
God coin? wake up

62229077
>>62228839
Every platform has risks

62229086
>>62225044
Wait for $1M and have a good life for the rest of your life

62229114
>>62229042
I don't trust "smart" contracts either for holding substantial funds long term. The most I've ever been willing to risk is like $50k on DeFi.

62229336
>>62229114
I know anon, but the newer native BTC infrastructure is built more around predefined protocol rules while still keeping self custody. And nobody’s saying you need to ape your entire stack into it either.

62229340
>>62228391
Sure, poorfag
>>62229086
I lost hope for it, i don't think I'll see that during my lifetime. I'll be lucky to get 300k price wise before 2035 kek.

62229342
>>62220158
He also got decent bags

62229433
>>62229340
Pass it to your children. Hodl

62229475
>>62219942
I will own productive assets instead sar.

62229508
>>62229475
What's that? Is there any?

62229551
>>62229433
I don't have kids, it's over
>>62229508
Sp500 is beating our ass daily, tomorrow is making another ath kek

62229557
>>62229114
Don't trust
>>62229551
You should be having kids if you're over 30 now. Think of your fortune if you have any

62229561
>>62229551
What about forex?

62229570
>>62229557
Yeah, don't think I'm getting kids anytime soon and blew it with one of the few women I might had.
>>62229561
I'm an europoor and i don't do forex trading. I just hold and dca my wagie paycheck, but BTC has been shit this whole market run. And yes the opportunity cost once again is making no sense. Why not pile everything into boomer stocks, better risk and reward after all. I might need to diversify my bags and forget about Bitcoin for a while.
Godspeed

62229578
>>62229570
Be loyal ser and start a good family

item.title: PIECE OF SHIT CRYPTO — 16 replies — 64.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
PIECE OF SHIT CRYPTO

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-05-14 10:19 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62228142
really dogshit at this point, even pokemon cards and ACTUAL POTATOES are doing better than btc.

62228149
I got rich by buying silver

62228162
I'll still make a profit if i could go back to 2011 and not spend those 70 bitcoins i bought with only 10% transaction fee from that based guido who had the best fuckin rates.
Back then people used ti charge you 25% or 30% for buying a btc.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62228166
crypto is going to go sideways for 20 years. Too many normgroids got in and all the og holders don't believe in it anymore

62228315
>>62228166

62228322
>>62228166
>all the og holders don't believe in it anymore
what about Max Keiser?

62228328
>>62228166
normgroids are gobbling up US stocks now

62228420
this is a good thing. bitcoin is supposed to counter-establishment so it should move against boomer index

62228426
My investments are up 7,000 USD today.

62229082
>>62228104
Ethers gravity is strong, it ll dumo further.....

62229102
>>62228166
No, a new narrative will happen soon and crypto will moon out of nowhere

62229360
>>62228104
See >>62225359

The top 10 is full of useless scams, while the key to actual crypto mass adoption is barely in the top 15.
This crypto market is all scammers, manipulators, and retards. Value investors are staying far away.

Until Link is rank 3, crypto will stay dead and stocks will continue to pump.

62229403
>>62228104
It literally doesn't do anything, or solve any problem.

Gambling on stocks is very easy now, and they've just got rid of the pattern day trader rule.

62229444
>>62229360
cope linkchud

62229465
>>62229360
>until LINK is rank 3
Link was rank 5 nearly 6 years ago stupid baggie

62229580
>>62229444
>>62229465
The stock market actually tends to price in news (bad and good), and tends to uphold relatively accurate market cap rankings based on current fundamentals and future potential.

The crypto market does not, so why would anyone invest?

item.title: Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio… — 36 replies — 43.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio…

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-05-14 09:39 UTC

OP

Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main question is at the bottom below the dashed line, so you can skip all of this text if you want.

Is anyone here good with statistics or math? Idk the correct field that would apply here, some math shit.
I’ve been working on a strategy for years at this point, but I’ve been focused on a single thing for about the last half year. I’ve back tested it till 2020 and its results are the photo. I wanted to use it with prop firms to make some cash since the potential profit with them is way higher than using my own cash, but the drawdowns are too severe. And prop firms are hella strict on their drawdowns.
I’ve run the math and if I scale down enough to where I can trade without blowing up my account, at the rate of withdrawals and taxes and splits, I’d be working for nothing. Min wage basically. And if I scale up then I just blow up accounts due to drawdown. I don’t care about profit either. I need smoothness. Even if I take a major hit in points, so long as it becomes smoother that’s still fine since I can just scale up on prop firms.

It’s basic metrics right now are
WR: 53.78%
Profit Factor: 1.36
Trades Per Day: 8.33

There’s a lot more data but that’s the gist.
The strategy is a blend so it’s RR varies, but it’s pretty close to 1:1RR

—————————-
Here’s my main question: the strategy currently averages 8 trades a day. That’s a lot. Surely I can cut that down to 1-2 and increase the winrate right, therefore increasing its smoothness right? Surely. I’ve tried a lot of things but I’ve failed all analytical approaches. I’ve done a lot of digging and I think I’m just out of my element here.

I think my request is gonna fall on deaf ears but I’m hopeful. Maybe someone earnest and smarter than me can help.

Early replies (first 3)

62220033
BUmp before I sleep

62220056
buy an ad grok

62222767
>>62220056
Does my post sound like an Ai made it lol? It’s a Genuine question

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62223406
>>62223260
>I’m also not sure how taking more trades with lower risk smooths equity
i explained this in my post, you encounter more luck-based variance on the upside and downside with lower trade volume. if your 95% quartile losing streak is 10-15 trades in a row you'll be in a smaller drawdown if your average risk per trade is smaller.

as a general rule, your win-rate and RR exist on a sliding scale. on one end you win 20% but its 5:1 RR and the other end you win 90% but its 0.25:1 RR. basically pick your poison and make sure your trading is actually consistent (not taking trades outside your system).

im a professional futures trader (CTA) and my system averages 35% win rate but 3:1 RR. i've used the same style to pass prop firm evaluations in the past but like you've eluded to, its not the best deal and you are limited by their specific rules. sometimes i have 60%+ win-rate over 1-2 weeks and other weeks i dont get a single win. overall i make money for my clients and return between 80%-120% annualized.

62223681
>>62223406
I’d definitely prefer to lean toward the higher winrate system. With prop firms, blowing accounts is kind of expected. Even if a strategy backtests badly long term, as long as it has strong win streaks before the inevitable losses, I feel like I can engineer it to pass evals, reach a payout, and make money before the losses hit.

So I don’t necessarily need something that wins long term. I need something that wins consistently enough to get to a payout. That difference feels important enough that there should be something workable there, no?

Even with my current strategy, it’s theoretically profitable over time, but like the screenshot shows, it can still go through drawdowns of a few hundred to a few thousand points. Unless I have a huge cushion on the prop firm account, there’s a good chance I blow the account before the edge plays out.

That’s why I prefer a high winrate system. If I can reach a payout first, I don’t really care if the account eventually blows after that. If I’m spending $100–$200 on an account plus resets, but can pull out $1k–$2k, then that’s worth it to me.

How come you have clients? Why not just trade for yourself and keep on scaling up?

62223765
>>62223681
sounds like you want to be a scalper, nothing wrong with that but its limiting for maximizing profits. whats difficult with scalping is being glued to the screen & being paranoid of missing trades by seconds.

If you're only trading NQ or ES mostly on the long side, of course backtesting & optimizing will tell you to just hold through drawdown because it always returns to all time highs. this is a phenomenon that only occurs in US stocks and its teaching you bad habits for trading. a good trader always minimizes losses when they're wrong and seeks to maximize profits when they're right. especially for a 1:1 RR system, its devastating to realize losing trades that are 2+ risk units from your average because it takes so many wins to gain that 1 big loss back.

>How come you have clients? Why not just trade for yourself and keep on scaling up?
even though i have clients, i basically have complete freedom and discretion for my trading within a certain defined range of the 8 markets i trade and risk levels. I write the rules I want. Im doing this as a career & everyone in the finance industry knows that you get rich with other people's money. most of my networth is invested along side my clients so im just gaining greater scale this way. I can bring on client accounts with millions of dollars way faster than what I can earn risking my own capital, the scaling is on another level.

62223860
>>62223765
Depends on what you mean by scalper. The strategies I’m using usually hold positions anywhere from 30 minutes to a few hours, sometimes the whole market day. They’re not super short-term in-and-out trades.

Since they’re rule-based and mechanical, the ideal setup would be to automate entries and step away. I’m not a fan of discretionary trading. I don’t need the whole pie, or even a slice. I just want a few crumbs and make some money and retire early.

>backtesting & optimizing will tell you to just hold through drawdown because it always returns to all-time highs
That is a valid concern but it doesn't apply to my strategy to my knowledge. One way I tested my strategy was through random 30-day simulations and pure 30-day sims.
Example:
Pick a random date walk forward 30 days while trading each day.
Or pick a random day and trade it pick another random day and trade it repeat for 30 days.

In both cases, the strategy was profitable overall. Around 75% of the sims ended green. The picture shows those sims.

If I had deep pockets, this would be simple: just run the strategy live and let it work. If I had traded just 1 NQ contract per trade since its inception around 5 years ago, I’d be looking at nearly $1M in profit, with the worst drawdowns around $40k–$50k. The math goes that I'd need about 50-100K to start, which is no small amount.

The issue is I don’t have that kind of capital. If I trade it properly with my current funds, I’d basically be making pennies. That’s why I need to make it work through prop firms at least to start out.

Again, I ain't asking anyone for money. I am asking if anyone here has knowledge with statistics and math, or some quantitative background. the strat takes 8 damn trades a day, surely there's some way to cut the fluff down to just a few high quality ones.

62224145
bump. y'all make posts about wanting an easy way out but no one puts in the work. ive been trying to make something work for years at this point and i feel like im on the cusp of something, but i realize what im looking for is outside my knowledge. i aint smart enough for this. ive tried so many methods but im hitting dead ends. i need the help of someone who's smarter than me here

62224160
>>62224145
I'm working on the same thing but I have no interest in helping anyone, why would I do that?

62224188
>>62224160
Fair point. The way I see it, I don’t mind if you know the strategy, especially if you end up helping me. That’s the least I could do.

My goal isn’t to milk it forever. I just want to use it to reach a point where I can retire and live a simple, happy life. After that, whatever happens to the strategies or lines doesn’t really matter to me. If you wanted to keep using them and scale them further, I’d be fine with that.

You mentioned you were working on something similar. How far did you get with it? What kind of metrics or performance were you seeing?

62224200
>>62219625
post pseudo code

62224207
>>62224200
the strategies are a mixture of VWAP and ORB. i used a base idea and iterated from there, testing different rule sets and requirements before deciding on the current setup. do you have a background in statistics or quantitative analysis?

62224225
>>62223860
holding for seconds or minutes at a time is usually a scalper. if you're holding for 30 mins to hours+ i dont think 1:1RR is efficient, there has to be some way to cut your risk spacing and achieve the same reward.

clearly you have some type of edge. you could either trade your own capital in MNQ contracts or try to make the drawdowns fit for the prop firm plan. you dont have to trade full size NQs you could have a lot more flexibility with MNQ.

>strat takes 8 damn trades a day, surely there's some way to cut the fluff down to just a few high quality ones
this is where you have to drill down to every trade taken and qualitatively sort out the lower quality trades. theres certainly nuances and details that differ across the trades taken so finding a better way to filter out the bad ones should yield better efficiency. you cant do that type of work through top down back-testing it has to be done trade by trade.

62224267
>>62224188
I have it working, it was running live for a few weeks. Made me an average 1% per day, but upon the first sign of danger I panicked and turned it off. Working on retooling it to notify me and I can configure it on the fly.

62224277
>>62224225

>there has to be some way to cut your risk spacing

Well anon, that’s basically the core problem. I don’t know how to qualitatively filter out lower-quality trades.

I’ve tried pulling data at the exact point of entry, things like EntryFromOpenPct, EntryFromVWAPPct, Prior10RangePct, Prior30ChurnPct, DayRangeSoFarPct, ES confirmation, EMA state, signal candle body/range, stop distance, target distance, planned RR, and a few other metrics.

The idea was to see if winners or losers had anything consistent in common, then build some kind of grading system to decide before entry whether a trade was worth taking. But that ended up being a dead end too. Although I am still testing variants of that as we speak with harsher filters, I'm not super hopeful.

I also don’t want to rely entirely on ChatGPT or AI to generate ideas, because it could easily lead me astray and I wouldn’t know, since I’m out of my element here. That’s why I’m stuck.

>you don’t have to trade full-size NQs, you could have more flexibility with MNQ

I looked into that too. The issue is that if I size down enough to survive the drawdowns, my scale becomes too small. It would take weeks or months to pass evaluations, then even more time to reach payouts.

And once I run the math, I’d be spending 3–5 hours a day trading for basically nothing. Not even $100/day in actual profit after costs and resets.

So technically, I do have a path to payouts right now. The problem is the math says it’s just not worth it.

>top down back-testing it has to be done trade by trade.

Sometimes if I have an idea, I’ll manually backtest it through a few days or weeks first. If it shows promise, then I’ll programmatically test it across the full dataset.

There are definitely some strategies that work well in specific regimes. The problem is that by the time you realize the regime has shifted, you’ve usually already given back most or all of what you made from it.

62224392
>>62224277
that sounds like the right path to be taking to find that extra quality filter. maybe you need more testing time to find that last filter piece. i've learned that more criteria is not always yielding more efficient results though, you can end up filtering out alot of good trades and reducing trade volume too much. i dont think AI is good for idea generation for trading or signals, personally i use AI to run all the math stats and simulations exclusively.

>would take weeks or months to pass evaluations, then even more time to reach payouts.
I passed most evaluations in 1 week to 8 weeks. i feel like if you're trying to pass in less than a week your size is prob too big. the 1 week pass was during a highly volatile period where i got much bigger wins.

It seems like you're on the right track. in trading, usually just doing 1 or 2 things really really well is better than having too many tools. i still disagree with your core stats of 1:1RR and requirements of high win-rate. i know theres traders that make that work but i've always wanted 1 big win to pay for 3+ losers. the big fish arent always biting but when you land 1 you realize chasing small fish all the time is a waste.

62224555
>>62224392
Everything you mentioned: sizing down, different contract sizes, different RR models, higher RR/lower win rate systems, and higher win rate/lower RR systems… I’ve already considered, tested, or looked into.

The main issue is the math. If it takes me around five weeks to pass a typical $50,000 evaluation with a $3,000 profit target, that works out to about $120 per trading day. If I then use the same strategy on a funded account, which makes sense since the rules are usually very similar, that same strategy is still only producing about $120 per day. After the prop firm split and taxes, that is well under $100 per day, which is not really viable long term or enough for financial independence.

The only way to make that work would be running multiple accounts, but that creates the same problem, just spread across many accounts all of which cost money to start and activate. If the strategy hits a deeper drawdown period, I could lose all of them at once.

So the issue is that I either size down so much that I spend thousands on evals, resets, and activation fees just to withdraw a few hundred dollars later, or I need to find a strategy that can grow more aggressively.

That is why I’m focused on a higher win rate + a higher profit factor. A lower win rate strategy might still have good EV on paper, but it also means more losing streaks and more resets along the way. A higher win rate gives me a better chance to build a cushion before those rough periods come.

With that being said, I’m not going to pretend I know everything. After all, I’m the one who came to this board asking for help.

The truth is, I don’t currently have a strategy with the kind of stats you’re describing (3:1RR) The one I have now, with its current win rate and profit factor, is the best I’ve been able to build so far hence why I’ve been sticking with it.

62224557
>>62224267
1% a day means 500% returns after just half a year. If you got a strategy like that then you’d be flying in a private jet within a few years lmao

62224754
>>62224555
>running multiple accounts, but that creates the same problem, just spread across many accounts all of which cost money to start and activate.
most prop firms let you have multiple accounts and if the economics are good enough for 1 you should always max the number of accounts for better scale. other prop firms just give you a bigger account (100k or 250k). yeah averaging $100 per day for 250 trading days per year is only $25k but ideally you get to 5x scale within the first 2 months then thats $125k per year. you can grind that out for years or find the next level of scale like trading for clients, another prop firm, or cut a deal with the prop firm you scaled up with.

idk if you're all in on this or its a side-job and you wage but its always about leverage past credibility & performance record to gain greater scale until you manage an uncomfortable amount of money.

62227720
>>62224754
>if the economics are good for one

Well anon, the economics AINT good enough for one hahaha. that's what I'm trying to solve. It's the question I asked in the very first post.

62228083
>>62224207
I am self taught and very well versed in quant the highest sharpe ive achieved is 1.9

62228084
>>62224557
that means hes larping and did the ting

62228111
>>62228084
It feels good to be doubted because what you've done amazes someone. Thanks for giving me the inspiration to keep at it.

62228122
>>62228083
Okay, here's my conundrum then: Suppose you have a list of 10K+ trades spanning from 2020 until now. Each trade has its data attached: date, entry, PnL, and any other metrics that might be important.

How would you go about finding some method or filter to cut out the low-quality trades from the high-quality trades? Ultimately, my strategy relies on momentum, so days where price chops heavily hurt me badly.

Perhaps I could find a way to either identify a chop day before it happens, or find a new addition or advanced criteria that would allow me to dynamically adjust my profit target and stop-loss based on what the model forecasts for that day.

Those are two ideas I have, but I haven’t been able to implement them simply because I’m not sure how to go about it.

I’ve checked the data and found that even the losing trades often have some degree of favorable movement before they eventually lose. That makes me wonder if I could find something that would tell me to reduce my profit target size for that day based on XYZ criteria.

Again, I’m kind of just spitballing because I genuinely don’t know where else to go with this. Everything I’ve tried to improve win rate by cutting losses has inevitably cut out winners as well. So I’m not sure what the right approach is.

62228424
bump

62228603
>>62228122
Low quality and high quality? That’s any MFT Strat lol

62228693
>>62228603
also if i help i get nothing from this

62228705
>>62228603
the strategy is not anything that would require low latency at least to my knowledge since the avg trade lasts anywhere from half an hour to several hours. again, i got no idea where to go from here. any tips would be appreciated

>>62228693
well if you helped me improve it, you could do whatever you want with it. It's not some proprietary strategy that requires some exotic data, it's pretty simple and generic. maybe that's why it's had a minor edge for so long. I would only be trading on it using prop firms, their sim accounts don't trade on live markets. and even if i did, i'm only trading NQ which is hella liquid. And as I've mentioned before, once I make a certain amount of money I'm done for good. Never gonna trade again. In theory if I could get this thing to a workable state, scaling it up would take a few months and I'd hit my goal within a year or two.

62228733
>>62224557
Depends on capital restrictions, an arbitrage or HFT strategy can easily get 1%/day but probably has limits on how much capital it can grow into.

62228952
>>62228733
lol

62228956
add 5% annual IBKR fees itll auto divide for margin or funding then 10 ticks of slippage

62229415
I still have yet to see someone who can help.

>>62228733
1% a day seems impossible in the long run. No strategy can have only good days with no bad days.

>>62228956
If they are buying and selling contracts to such an extent then fees would def eat into it. But if it’s a single buy or sell, or a position that’s held for hours then I don’t see why not.

62229502
Bump

item.title: This is getting ridiculous — 25 replies — 20.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
This is getting ridiculous

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-05-14 09:15 UTC

OP

MSFT is currently trading at January 2024 prices. That’s 2.5 years of zero gains. Meanwhile the company is one of the largest and best in the world and growing in the high teens every year. I can’t see how this isn’t a screaming buy right now. I recognize the uncertainly surrounding their investment into AI, and possibly the negative sentiment around software stocks in general but for me Microsoft isn’t going anywhere

Early replies (first 3)

62228288
>>62228282
i bought around $400. i also bought META yesterday.

MA and V are good buys now also.

62228292
>>62228282
I have an average price per share of $366 I just keep buying along with Amazon Google and Apple.

62228297
>>62228288
V is a screaming buy but I don’t know enough about stable coins to assess the proper risk to the credit card business yet.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62228304
>>62228282
when it dropped to like $360 or whatever, i still didn't buy this piece of shit

62228309
>>62228282
your money could've been making more money buying mu and sndk at the top than holding this crap

62228317
>>62228282
openAI is a pile of shit

62228321
>>62228309
Yessir

62228343
windows is the boomer os thats being jeeted to hell, and everyone can see the writing on the wall. its like everytime a new windows version drops the screams of dissatisfaction get louder. Thats what happens after 20 years of letting indians nepo hire more of their kind. everyone is leaving azure for AWS too. gen alpha tr00ns will all be vibecoding on mac minis and lenovos running fedora. think of how many PowerBI boomers are retiring in the next decade. why even bother with x86 when the day of the pillow finally arrives and frees us all.

62228415
>>62228297
A company trading at a PE of around 28 is screaming buy? It's only down like 10% in the last year.

62228438
>>62228282
The only thing Microsoft does well is datacenter hosting. Everything else they do is shit.

They make their gains through shady business practices like dark patterns and built in spyware or buying out and shutting down the competition. Not by having a best in class product and marketing it well.

You need to take all of those things into consideration before going long on MSFT.

62228448
Reason the share price is stagnant is cuz all of the people laid off there got their RSUs early and have cashed out.

62228452
The market (chiefly AI) is fully saturated with all the monopoly money anyone is currently willing to risk. Things have to start dying for other things to achieve "fair value"

62228457
>>62228282
Culmination of Azure getting mogged by AWS and ChatGPT (Microsoft is balls deep in OpenAI) losing ground to Gemini and Claude.

62228475
>>62228415
They are a digital payment toll keeper and the bridge is already built a small amount of maintenance capital expenditure per year and you are set.

62228486
>>62228282
If AI fails to boost economic growth as much as the markets expect then tech stocks crash but if it does then software loses value.
MSFT might be worth buying starting at around 200-250. Currently indexes are overheated on the HTFs which makes long-term investments risky.

62228488
>>62228415
one day /biz/ will learn to look at the 1 year forward PE and growth rate

62228489
>>62228486
its already boosting economic growth

62228502
>>62228282
>Meanwhile the company is one of the largest and best in the world
their main product is worthless
you don't need any office products, you can interact with them online/ through a FOSS or through your agent/LLM
you also don't make them yourself anymore

microsaar has been dumping money into openAI but they will not be getting any equity
they're on track to become IBM 2

62228524
>>62228489
Real GDP YoY isn't spiking at all. Should be like 5% to justify the parabolic market rally.

62228537
>>62228282
>5% linkedin layoffs
>20-30% of some teams wiped company wide
>ticker didn't even twitch
lmao macroshart

62228596
Designated shitting company

62228906
Microsoft is history. We are going to see at least 80%-90% downside from here. Nothing is going to save this.

62228910
Also notice Nadella wasn’t invited to China. He’s the biggest embarrassment in the tech sector.

62228937
>>62228282
idk pal, if that was a coin would you buy this chart?

62229462
>>62228282
So well positioned after turning their OS into a Linux bootloader with extra spyware and renaming their flagship Office products after a shit chatbot and making them rolling updates so no longer stable either as UI or backwards compatibility.
Do they still do the Xbox thing?
Only positive I can find is that the pivot to AI will optimize their enshittification workload and remove billions of jeets but I don't know how that can be monetized.

62229516

62229560
>>62229483

item.title: Bitcoin or altcoins? — 92 replies — 19.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 92 • Updated: 2026-05-14 05:43 UTC

OP

If you had to pick, what would you buy right now: BTC or altcoins? I’m not seeing much ROI in alts lately, even utility or memecoins.

Early replies (first 3)

62195293
>>62195275
Bitcoin until 120k

62195300
you can't time the market I thought like >>62195293
last time, bitcoin to 70k and sell half and go into alts and then 2021 alts just fucking died for a year.

The alts that pumped are things that haven't gone parabolic yet, or big projects like Solana with glowie money attached to them. Ones like Link are not going to 1000 regardless of cope.

/biz/ will pretty much only give you bad info too. I've found maybe 3 coins here that were Guaranteed returns, everything else I had to research myself. In fact I remember last year posting proof that CRCL stock was going to 10x on IPO and got XRP schizos fudding me out of taking a life changing position.

I actually hate the fuck out of baggies.

62195373
>>62195293
Why 120k only?? Why not 1M?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62209376
>>62207965
Let's see how it goes

62209379
>>62204588
So are you into btc?

62209385
Bitcoin is getting fucked

62209525
>>62209385
Utility coins barely move unless the market gets euphoric again. Just btc

62209590
People pretending fundamentals matter in crypto will never stop being funny.

62209854
>>62195275
Everyone becomes a macro analyst after holding one red candle lmao

62210047
>>62209304
but when?

62210050
>>62209291
hold and stake

62211194
>>62207855
I've got some hbar in my portfolio

62211429
>>62211194
hbar going down to 2c before it ever gets a chance at real traction

62211440
>>62195275
>Chainlink is the future

62211870
>>62211440
Hahaha

62211903
>>62195275
Everyone moved to BTC of course, I dumped my ETH for BTC too, I used to hold BTC because of staking but now I can stake BTC natively thanks to TBV and the likes

62211915
>>62195275
I’d rather keep stacking BTC, then put it to work via native staking / yield layers (like Babylon-style setups) instead of chasing dead charts and hoping for a narrative pump.

62211928
>>62209385
Ok as you say so

62211932
>>62211429
Hbar is okay to hold and buy

62212034
>>62195275
More potential upside on alts for the next six months. Specifically memes right now.

62213245
>>62211440
What's your price target anon?

62214696
>>62211932
All in or?

62216973
>>62211915
It's worth considering anon

62216987
>>62195275

Polkadot

62220595
>>62209854
Are you sure?

62224657
>>62216987
Why polkadot?

62224666
cny/usd is > .147 now w/trump headed to china. every penny that can chase risk on is gonna get horned up immediately. The ONLY thing the US has left is the dollar as a w3@p0n. Plus, i can't afford any more makers mark bottles unless my alts start pumping w/haste, so...

62224894
>>62224666
Crazy

62224972
>>62224666
We're cooked

62226444
>>62224666
Risk-on driven by geopolitics, liquidity, and alcoholism. Peak crypto posting.

62228393
>>62216987
>ICP

62228841
chainlink

62229090
>>62216987
he's also pedo

item.title: The BIGGEST Crypto Integration in History — 63 replies — 15.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
The BIGGEST Crypto Integration in History

Open thread • Replies: 63 • Updated: 2026-05-14 06:19 UTC

OP

Historic moment as Chainlink services have been selected to be used by the DTCC! This is monumental to the crypto and blockchain industry. They even pinned the tweet! Wow, to think how far we have come as an industry.

Early replies (first 3)

62224616
Is this good or bad?

62224627
when are people going to wake up and realize that chainlink is the only crypto that matters? when will the fucking price finally go up?

62224642
>>62224627
I think you honestly really needed the spoon feeding from autists here back in 2017 to gather the confidence we have in Link. By the time Link got on the crypto map in 2020 or whenever people were cemented in their cliques for whatever coin they believed in. Most of the crypto space are normalfaggots. I’ve gotten a handful of people to buy, but you can never tell them to go all in cuz they are not emotionally invested in link. I got6 people to invest 300, 300, 700, and 500 and 400 and 1700. Almost all of them

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62225215
>>62225100
whew, thanks asswipe i really needed that, now i feel like i'm back to reality, i ain't sellin, i'm just gonna baghold another decade i guess

62225318
>>62224577
Chainlink? Now that’s a name I’ve haven’t heard in a long long time I had some when I was a young man.

62225326
WOW! This is huge!

Now let me just check the price and the chart…oh…

62225449
>>62225181
because the conversation about link's potential is like schizo babble

62226061
>>62224577
I thought this happened like 2 years ago..

62226079
>>62225318
im 25y and will be a millionaire before 30 with my linkstack, stay seething

62226196
>>62224749
>9 years of bagholding
Hey cuckold, check the price :)

62226241
>>62226196
thx i just checked oh boy! am I le excite!

62226533
>*dumps*

62227022
can I get an honest and unbiased QRD on link?

62227259
>>62227022
No, I don't think you can. Read the white papers and think for yourself. That'll probably be the closest you can get. Everyone else who would answer you is emotionally invested one way or the other. Me? I think it's the only project that matters and that it'll make me the equivalent of a billionaire one day. It's the next iteration of the internet, just as DLT was the next iteration of the computer.

62227290
>>62227022
No.

62227293
>>62224577
>dumps to single digits

62227302
>>62224749
Bag size?

62227385
>>62227022
Fundamentally, I’ve noticed a lot of new posters in the last 69hr (SIXTY NINE) asking about Chainlink and just wanted to warn you. Chainlink is shilled HEAVILY here and it’s very hard to understand so this background is crucial to ensure you stay safe.

Basically, a bunch of RACIST WHITE CIS MALE 4channers tried to force it as /ourcoin/, during the presale ico phase of chainlink there was a minimum requirement of 300eth to enter the presale.

Coin continued to get shilled and pumped up and hyped for the sibios event that link was attended, whole event turned out to be a flop chainlink had a presentation in a room of like 18 people (IT WAS BESIDE THE FUCKING TOILETS FOR FUCK'S SAKES, YOU COULD HEAR THEM FLUSHING), literally no news or partnership came from the event.

Now during this alt bull run lots of anons and took advantage of this and shilling this coin to all the new money and newfags that joined in december and don't know this story.
The coin is HEAVILY manipulated by a literal TRANNY DISCORD who pump and dump this coin more than their boyfriends pump and dump hot cum into their asses, and the supply is dried up from huge fucking chink whales (xi jinping is rumoured to hold link) who accumulated below ICO price to create a artificially lower supply.

In regards to actual project that chainlink aiming to achieve it's nothing more than a basic json parser (OH MY GOD ITS JSON PARSER...) for smart contracts (LOL HOW CAN A CONTRACT BE "SMART"???), would take like a day to add to ethereum by itself.. literally making links whole concept pointless and definitely no need for a token. You're literally just buying a wh*tepaper.

They only have 2 devs who are literal pajeets who are payed in big macs (non beef) and Surgay NAZAJEET is a literal psychologist (trusting a shrink? is he gonna tell us to go clean our room next?). So FUNDAMENTALLY, it's a scam, ok thanks.

Now go do the needful and fundamentally buy a real coin with fundamentals like XRP.

62227397
>>62227385
I can't tell whether gatekeeping normies is good or evil. I go back and forth

62227410
>>62226079
Me too Son, just a little later, a-and besides 10 years is nothing . Just hold on WGMI

62227441
>>62227397
Normies already live on easy mode, we live on nightmare difficulty.

62227473
>>62224616
bad, sell the news event + token not needed
>>62224627
chainlink might matter, LINK does not

>>62224974
many ICO bros made it back in 2020 at $50 withtheir 50-70k stacks, sure they're no warrent buffets but it was enough to live comfortably, too bad most didn't sell

>>62225125
yeah, as long as the tokens aren't needed you can have top of the NASDAQ sucking Chainlinks dick, people just stopped caring, it's not 2017 anymore. Back then it would've sent LINk to triple digits, but those days are long gone, peoeple wanna see actual demand nowadays, but it ain't coming, because Chainlink /= LINK

62227498
>>62224863
Sui has always been 1k

62227508
>>62227498
jesus dude that's like $10k, even if LINK does a 3x (and that's being generous) you'd only have $30k, what are you gonna do with that? lol

62227538
SERGEY BETRAYS US

SELL SELL FOR YOUR LIVES

62227556
>>62224958
This anon is correct.

62227563
>>62227508
Link will be 1k+

62227593
>>62227563
No it will pump to $30 and dump back down to $5.

62229154
>>62224577
it's big news!

62229156
>>62229154

62229158
>>62229156

62229164
>>62229158
Is that fat bitch* still fudding?

*the woman not Sergey

62229165
>>62229164
She's Nadine Chakar from the DTCC and she's partners with Chainlink

item.title: Taxes Thread — 24 replies — 9.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Taxes Thread

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-05-14 08:00 UTC

OP

Can any USA tax lads please go more in depth and in detail about how to set up a tax efficient business+trust?

Particularly how to be the most tax efficient with a business that is also protected by within a trust? S-Corp or C-Corp? Both in the same state? Offshore? How do you avoid double taxation?

Most importantly, how would you invest personal and business income in long term stocks that you select?

How would you deal with income you got from an actual job somewhere which is not part of the your business but that you wanted protected by a trust and invested in your choice of stocks?

What would all the target tax rates be?

Please detail everything. Provide tips and cover all risks, etc. I have tried studying this on my own but the scenario I want in which all income from business and personal in custom selected stocks while protected by trust and taxed as small as possible, complicates things.

Early replies (first 3)

62218156
>>62218140
consult a great tax attorney and a cpa

62218211
>>62218140
if you make a personal s-corp you can expense everything in CA like paying for your own health insurance for example but you pay a minimum franchise tax of $800 even if you made nothing so not great for side-hustles owned by me that made nothing

62218357
>>62218140
Formula F1. If you can spare 10m, sponsor an F1 team and then expense your entire life

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62218371
>>62218140
Totally off-topic but it really makes me seethe that I’m really out here paying 25 percent of my income to pedophiles, then on top of that I get taxed on investments, purchasing anything, property, etc. Our ancestors tarred and feathered tax collectors over a 2 percent tax on stamps and now we work for three months out of the year for the government.

62218405
>>62218211
This is basically the grift. You set up an S-Corp and deduct everything as a “business expense.”

It is also illegal to claim a business deduction for personal expenses, but this never stopped black people on TikTok from expensing the lease payments on their Chryslers.

62218495
>>62218140
I hate redditors and normies and low iq's. Pic related is such a retarded analogy. The company gets tax deductions because those expenditures were related to income generation. A person can do the same thing by getting tax deductions on things deemed necessary for work or income generation. You spending your income on overpriced doordash is not related to income generation.

62218507
>>62218357
Yeah just pay 10m to save 4m in taxes goy. Not a very smart jew. This is only worth it if you were going to spend money to live that lifestyle anyway, or you needed the marketing regardless.

62218524
the business spent that 3.9B on salaries to all the retards making 60k

62218651
I paid like 95k in income tax then got clocked for 4k more when I did my taxes.

62218760
>>62218140
Unless you make serious cash, this is the best you can hope for.

62218844
>>62218495
huh? i can't work hungry

62218962
>>62218140
Talk to @TheJerzWay on X (he will require payment), but he's a pro. He'll probably also recommend getting your REPS and residency in Puerto Rico.

62219425
>>62218140
Just don't pay your taxes
Most of them go towards jews and the rest goes towards trying to bring in as many third world subhumans to replace you as possible
It is probably the most cucked thing you can do in the current age, especially in the U.S
Anyone who disagrees with this should respond with "I am a good goy cuck" because there's no rationalisation or argument to make what I'm saying any less true.

62219490
>>62218140
>That's not–
You don't pay taxes at all, do you?
You don't know what a Standard Deduction is, do you?
>File by May 15th
Yeah, whoever wrote this has never paid taxes in their life.
>>62218507
>This is only worth it if you were going to spend money to live that lifestyle anyway,
He was. They all do. This is "Hollywood accounting". But now he can use his business to fund it with no risk to his personal assets.

62219495
>>62218844
Your basic needs are covered by the standard deduction.

62220996
>>62218357
Good job shlomo!

62221525
>>62218140
Tell me you don't know the fucking difference between a W2 and a 1099 without telling me.

62223970
>>62218405
Ben affleck explains how to do this in the Accountant movie.

62224000
>>62218405
Yes, but ultimately this just lets you live an inflated lifestyle at a discount. If you don't really want a G wagon, even making it tax deductible doesn't really help because you've still got to pay for the non deductible amount. And if you don't have a business that lets you pretend like maybe you need it (ie. A real estate agent taking wealthy clients to see homes)

62224006
>>62218140
>60k income
>58k expenses
yeah no wonder the person who made this meme doesen't understand taxes

62226707
>>62221525
I don't know the fucking difference between a W2 and a 1099.

62229176
>>62226707
landlords in image kek

62229320
>>62218357
AI slop.

62229358
>>62226707
>landlords
Too obvious, I am not sure you'll get anyone.
Funny, though.

item.title: >redditors are getting rich and posting their 1M dollar screenshots from investing in RKLB and AMD >everyone here is poo… — 36 replies — 8.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
>redditors are getting rich and posting their 1M dollar screenshots from investing in RKLB and AMD >everyone here is poo…

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-05-14 06:17 UTC

OP

>redditors are getting rich and posting their 1M dollar screenshots from investing in RKLB and AMD
>everyone here is poor as fuck

Early replies (first 3)

62208450
just goes to show 4chan is much more retarded than >leddit :^)

62208458
only a few more weeks until I can post on reddit

62208475
>>62208412
We were targeted specifically by kikes to be made poor

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62208528
>>62208412
>>everyone here is poor as fuck
Everyone here is the most retarded kind of skeptic, bagholder fudding new investments, or bear who thinks the market is going back to 30k on the DJIA for nk reason.

62208530
>>62208501
>>62208497
>>62208475
Reddit doesn't ruthlessly purge The Next Big thing until it's too late to make sizable gains, it doesn't have low IQ browns phoneposting "SAAR BUY THE TOP OF A MASSIVE CANDLE" it doesn't have 50yo xoomers fudding every good investment becuase "muh tech bubble was bad" 20 years ago.

62208534
>>62208528
kek you are poor

62208537
>>62208458
my ip is range banned from reddit permanently

62208538
Don't kid yourselves. Crypto is in the beginnings of a decade long bear market. /pmg/fags will get the final laugh.

62208544
>>62208528
you keep taking my screenshot. I want to strangle you

62208568
>>62208530
Reddit does, in fact, purge a lot of its comments. It is also filled to the brim with botposting. Don't know why you'd treat their circlejerk as more valuable than any other random shitpost you read online.

>>62208544
I should take it and use it as mine too.

62208833
>>62208412
I see WSB getting filthy rich meanwhile /biz/ chuds are here bagholding chainlink and "investing" in rocks

62209921
>>62208412
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62209936
>>62208501
the weird part here is we have bears that never post ANY gains LOL

62211249
>>62208537
based
>>62209921
go post that over there

62213463
>>62208412
I'm doing ok

62217703
>>62208412
why is this thread still up its been 2 days wtf

62218664
>>62209921
>take a $1,000,000 loan

Suddenly a millionaire.

62218683
>>62213463
give me 100k and you'll fix my life

62218698
>>62208530
I remember the dot com crash
>/b/ told me pets.com the make it stock

62218792
>>62208458
So what will you do with the money once it reaches 1 million, will it be enough for you?

I wont be satisfied until I reach at least 3 million which is 10-12 years away at a minimum.

62218804
>>62208530
reddit has loser bears too

62221909
if I were to sell everything today I'd have 20K€

62222773
>>62221909
What were you doing for the past 10 years? if you saved 25-30k a year youd have at least over 400k by now from the compounded interest.

62222950
>>62218792
I already have over a million. I am an oldfag already, so I am aiming for 2.5 or so before I consider retiring. with retirement accounts and cash I am around 1.6. realistically if I retire early my biggest cost will be health insurance. I don’t want to risk going without in my late 40s and 50s.

62222978
Fine. I give up. This place is a total shit hole and I’ll never make it by browsing this total brown cesspit. What subreddits do I go to make money?

62223083
>>62222950
Ill live long. This is my affirmation. I will live long, healthy, and get to 3 million dollars by the time I'm 35.

62223085
>>62208495
retard doesnt know what survivorship bias is. I have 100% called the last 3 market bottoms because every reddit post was screaming about an imminent market collapse

62225427
>>62222773
>the past 10 years
been working for 3, was a neet before
>25-30k
I make that a year before taxes, you probably missed the euro sign

62225585
>>62209936
I have nothing

62226924
>>62208412
Fuggg I was thinking of buying AMD a few years ago when NVIDIA was going apeshit, but I did some googling and something I read convinced me not to. Forgot what it was. Feels batman

62226971
>>62218683
No

62228746
>>62208458
>web designer is too retarded to make an actual line graph chart, it's a bunch of fat rectangles stitched together.

62229161
>>62221909
So top 1% in Europe?

item.title: Australia is fucked — 24 replies — 4.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Australia is fucked

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-05-14 05:19 UTC

OP

>CGT discount - DENIED
>Negative Gearing - DENIED
Where were you when the Australian dream died?

Early replies (first 3)

62224090
have the liberals said they will reverse it? do we just hold and wait?

62224122
>>62223850
Doesn’t this come into effect in 2027? Should I sell all my crypto before 2027?

62224148
>>62223850
its tiring living here
the new rules favour low return long duration assets. high growth assets like btc and leveraged etfs get smoked compared old rules. run a few tests... under 12 months nothing changes.
i wonder if it actually encourages speculation. might as well punt on options or asx bets miners or U.S letfs... you need BIG wins now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224204
>>62223850
Negative gearing has always been retarded.

62224281
>>62223850
Explain whats changed so i can care or not ?
t aussie

62224288
>>62223850
>the Australian dream
KEK
Australia was a prison land where we sent the scum to so they can starve or kill each other.
Only after the jews mettled did they get rights.

62224331
>>62224281
No more long term CGT discount. Minimum taxed is now always 30% even if you used to be in lower bracket

62224341
>>62224288
>where we sent
I really hope this isn't a bong posting, because lol if you think bongs are in any position to be talking like this in current year.

62224346
>>62224288
Shalom!

62224365
Relax, the changes only apply to new purchases, as long as you already have your properties you'll be paying less tax than future property owners.

62224398
>>62224346
>doesn't even reach half a billion
LOL

62224409
CGT changes for all asset types is retarded and makes it so property is still the best investment
My cope is that negative gearing changes will result in cheaper properties but higher yields as rental supply drops

62224440
>>62224365
dont care about properties, i want to know if shares are grandfathered in

62224467
>>62223850
SOMEONE EXPLAIN THIS TO ME IN ENGLISH
JUST HOW COOKED ARE WE CUNTS???

62224496
>>62224331
Right thanks m8.

62224499
>>62224440
This, except crypto and boomer rocks.

62224536
>>62224467
Return to pre-1999 CGT system.

62224566
>>62224536
CGT is theft, Fuck you albo.

62224682
>>62223850
I voted for this.

62224702
>>62224536
but... w why they do this do this to us

62224712
I'm of two minds on one hand I have about 600k AUD of cgt obligations which if I were to use the discount while I can would have to lock in maybe 100k of tax this coming year (yes I'll be at 45% bracket because I'm not a brokie)

On the other hand I'm priced out of the property market (not really but I don't want to pay 900k for a shitty Sydney apartment) that any fucking over of property investors is actually a good thing (for me and people my age)

62224719
>>62224712
Doesnt making $600k in profit automatically put you in the highest tax bracket owed regardless of how much you earned from waging?

62226023
Honestly, everyone should be sticking their money into classic cars, right this instant.

Buy a final edition V8 Landcruiser 70 series, a MK V Supra or a Y62 Patrol Warrior and just sit on it.

Watch this space, its exempt from CGT and may sky rocket in value, especially as we push forward into cucked out 4cyl and 3cyl hybrid cuckboxes.

My cars will now become my investments, fuck the Government.

62229029
>>62226023
retro games for me. if you spend less than 500 dollars on a collectible item it's tax-exempt when you sell it. they have more universal appeal than pokemon cards and many well known games are becoming extremely rare to find in complete condition.

item.title: OOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO — 1 replies — 4.0/hr

Open thread • Replies: 1 • Updated: 2026-05-14 10:27 UTC

OP

OOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

Early replies (first 3)

62229593
lol

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Labour shortage — 21 replies — 3.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Labour shortage

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-05-14 04:22 UTC

OP

How is there a labour shortage if you apply on indeed and there's 2000 people applying for the same job? For every 300 jobs you apply you get 1 interview and they act like you've giving an interview for the position of the CEO of a transnational mega corporation. Why are they so fucking picky if there's a labour shortage? I remember giving a interview to be a bus boy and I told the interviewer that I thought I'd find the job easy and he got angry and said it was very difficult job. I never got called back. I worked a dead end job for 2 years and somehow I got it while applying for another job initially. You had to be able to work between anytime from 5am to 1am and work 60 hours a week and almost everyone working there was an asylum seeker living in hotels who had come on student visas. They were all planning on either saving up enough money and then going back to their countries or planned on entering the US illegally and getting bank loans (free money) from american banks. I quit the job since it was going no where and I needed a better paying job. Now I'm looking for a job for the summer before I start my last year of college but I am reminded of how fucked the job market is.

Early replies (first 3)

62224241
>>62224230
I have 70k CAD. How can I third world max?

62224247
>>62224230
who told you there was a labor shortage

62224252
>>62224247
The government telling me the reason for why we have mass migration. Apparently it's due to a labour shortage.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224271
>>62224252
there's a labor shortage of farm workers, meat processors, etc. jobs that you won't do.

62224278
>>62224230
There is no shortage, the corporations just want to pay less for labor so they get the government to flood the market with turd worlders.
>>62224271
I'd pack meat for the right price, problem is they don't wanna pay that price. If I offered you the job of sucking my dick for $5 a day, and you refused, suddenly there's a labor shortage.

62224391
>>62224278
>the right price
you mean the market price?

62224410
There's a skilled labor shortage and it makes things annoying in the blue collar world. But it's also very lucrative if you have the skills and an ounce of financial discipline.

62224415
>>62224230
This image depicts hundreds of asylum seekers lining up outside the Jacob K. Javits Federal Building in New York City.NYC Migrant Crisis: The scene illustrates the overwhelming influx of migrants straining New York City's infrastructure and shelter system.Housing Challenges: In response to the crisis, city officials have utilized various locations for housing, including tent cities on Randalls Island, hotels, and a former police academy.Policy Changes: NYC has abandoned its blanket "right to shelter" policy, implementing 30- and 60-day notices for adult migrants to find alternative housing.

62224442
>>62224410
>skilled labor shortage
In what fields?

62224717
This is why we need Indian CEOs.

62224883
When hiring managers start getting luigi'd, things will get better.

62224884
>>62224442
the fields that won;'t hire you because the flip side of skilled labor and experience shortages is businesses refusing to hire and train more labor.

62225124
>>62224410
>But it's also very lucrative if you have the skills and an ounce of financial discipline.
so which blue collar jobs are paying $150k/yr for a 40 hour work week?

62225164
There isn't a labour shortage, it's called obscuring the start of a depression.

62225251
>>62224391
"The market price" isn't the price of the job being done in bangladesh you illiterate third worlder. The market price is how much the job is worth in the country it's being offered. That's why companies are basically lying to the SEC to run a massive fraud/pyramid scheme to draw in as many migrants who will work for that price as possible.

62225256
>>62224410
The "Skilled labor shortage" is again based on fraud and malcontent. Their labor shortage is that you won't work for third worlder prices, nor should you. So they lie to the SEC to claim they are suffering a labor shortage so they can get H1Bs to do the job, and then everything falls apart because their job experience is fraudulent as well.

62228510
>>62225251
fuck off commie
the market is the market. wages are subject to market rates
>NOOO YOU HAVE TO PAY ME MORE BECAUSE... YOU JUST HAVE TO, OKAY?
>GIBS ME DAT
i am going to drop you from a helicopter

62228612
It is within the interest of a company to always say that there's a labor shortage.
If you don't know why this is, you need to think a bit harder.

62228761
>>62228510
You're being dishonest. Mass immigration is class warfare. It's an intentional saturation of the labor market to depress wages and increase the margins of the ruling class.

62228806
>>62228761
>class warfare
Its race warfare.

62228925
>>62228761
>class warfare

62224311

62224318
>>62224311

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224592
>>62224292
New fagshit dropped oooooooooooooooooo

62224594
>>62224592
Sry forgot pic

62224597
>>62224292
>''american'' billionaires fly to China to beg in person for them to loose up their asses even by little
everything they possibly offer technologically and resource wise is already in China, unless they offer up (you) and chinamen will go for it for some reason. And Taiwan too but that's not a "deal" that's hanging up white flag and letting go of US influence in east asia

62224617
>>62224292
that's why I don't envy these CEOs that much. imagine having to share a private plane with smelly donald trump just to get humiliated in china by people who prepared 1000 times better for this meeting than the trump administration is capable of. all that while they certainly have "the cards" and while you have to agree with a dementia patient that the USA is outperforming everyone in everything. literally nobody wants to be on that trip.

62225118
>>62224318
Liberals are now actually believing digits on 4chan mean something.

Glorious.

62225176
Did he really say Tim Apple again kek

62225297
>>62224431
who invites a man before throwing him from a plane?

62225310
>>62225176
Tim Apple is hilarious.

62225504
>>62224292
>Tim Apple

Imagine being Tim Cook and being called Tim Apple hahahhaa what a fucking cuck

62225529
>Tim Apple
>Larry Blackrock
>Elon Tesla
>Jensen Nvidia

62225538
>>62224311
Ahhhhhhhh it’s fucking happening!!!!!!!!!!!

62225699
>>62224292
Him and Elon are friends again?

62225749
How like is to Nvidia reach 10 Trillion in Marketcap in the next 2 years?

62225825
>>62225699
the is no friendship in these circles, only loyalty. Elon learned his lesson.

62225943
>>62225297

They are expecting one of us in the wreckage brother.

62225964
>>62225699
I see you don't know how pro-wrestling works. Elon has done a "double turn" where he went from babyface to heel and now he is back to babyface.

62226072
>>62225964
this wise anon knows that if you're a smark who understands kayfabe, you're automatically fluent in politics

62226167
>>62224414
Right? This seems like some sort of last ditch effort, and almost a recognition that China "won" in some greater sense. Actually pretty disappointing we've reached this point, cowtowing to communists

62226338
>>62226167
Ironically "kowtowing" is a Chinese term

62226357
>>62224292
lol left wing media btfo again

62226359
how do i profit from this

62226366
>>62226359
Buy Nvidia I guess. He's clearly going to allow Jensen to sell to them

62226395
>>62226366
I checked the CEOs travelling with him. Why does he have a random small/mid-cap biotech company with him? Does anyone know anything about Illumina? Its only 22 billion mcap doing DNA sequencing

62226412
>>62226395
Theres a new plandemic remember. they're probably there to help China combine the bat flu with the hantavirus.

62226422
>>62224324
How do i get out of this chickenshit market?

62226424
>>62224292
is this why metals are going up

62226428
>>62225749
>all that bloated garbage

62226472
>>62226424

62226813
>>62224292
>Selling out our country to our biggest geopolitical rival for cheap economic gain
>Bringing our biggest geopolitical rival to a "higher level"
Wow! Based! So fucking MAGA!

62227717

BOBO looks like it will reach 20m soon

Surely the og BOBINA has a chance to reach 1m

rite guise?

62224478

62224515

62224530
>>62224447

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224534

62224539
>>62224530
>>62224534
It is the first one which means the other ones are a copy

62224543
>>62224539

62224548

62224558

62224560

62224573

62224579

62224595

62224680

62224706

62224710

immediate izzat loss here, we can smell you through the screen

62224770
When is shitcoin season unironically going to come back? I need money for whores

62227926
>>62224573
No the team that made this (Bobina on ETH) actually has artwork of her and PSDs, plus the Bobo deployer bought and they are followed by them I don’t see shit in comparison to this shitter just looks like trash imho. I think Vibe is a piece of shit personally but I gotta give some credit where it’s due him and his group of faggots made a lot and that’s an understatement busted quite a few nuts.

62228626
>>62227926

62228734
>>62228626
Google just shows a bunch of furry wolf girl shit, where can I fap to this faggot.

62228747
>>62224447
If söylana memes are back then that means the rest of crypto is fucked forever.

62228751
>>62224447
Solana memecoins are doing something.
TROLL is up 600% over the last month.
24% today. I know some of you here have traded this in the past... I hope you held some!

The big news:
>As of mid-2026, the $TROLL meme coin on the Solana network has secured exclusive intellectual property rights to the iconic "Trollface" meme, transforming it from an unofficial meme token into a licensed brand.
